title: rhododendron arboreum subsp. arboreum
intro: Rhododendron arboreum subsp. arboreum, also known as the tree rhododendron, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia, characterized by its large, showy flowers and broad, elliptical leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been shown to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. Rhododendron arboreum subsp. arboreum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used in salves and ointments to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
